## Link Rendering Architecture: Rich Editor & Static Renderer

### Overview

Links in the Shiroi editor ecosystem use two distinct mechanisms:

1. **Inline links** — rendered as `<a>` tags via Lexical's `LinkNode` and `AutoLinkNode`
2. **Link card blocks** — custom `LinkCardNode` that displays rich link previews with favicon, title, description, and image

---

### 1. Inline Links

#### Editor Side (Rich Editor)

Links are handled by Lexical's built-in nodes, configured in `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/config.ts`:

```typescript
import { AutoLinkNode, LinkNode } from '@lexical/link'

export const builtinNodes = [
  LinkNode,
  AutoLinkNode,
  // ... other nodes
]
```

**Two link types:**

| Node Type | Source | Behavior |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| `LinkNode` | Lexical | Explicit inline links created by user (text + href) |
| `AutoLinkNode` | Lexical | Auto-detected URLs and email addresses |

**Auto-detection** via `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/AutoLinkPlugin.tsx`:

```typescript
// Matches URLs and emails automatically
const URL_REGEX = /https?:\/\/(?:www\.)?[-\w@:%.+~#=]{1,256}\.[a-zA-Z]{2}[-\w()@:%+.~#?&/=]*/
const EMAIL_REGEX = /(?:[\w.%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,})/i

export function AutoLinkPlugin({ matchers }: AutoLinkPluginProps) {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
  useEffect(() => {
    return registerAutoLink(editor, {
      matchers: matchers ?? DEFAULT_MATCHERS,
      changeHandlers: [],
      excludeParents: [],
    })
  }, [editor, matchers])
  return null
}
```

#### Static Renderer Side

Links are rendered in `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-static-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx` (lines 111-134):

```typescript
case 'link':
  return (
    <a
      key={key}
      className="rich-link"
      href={node.url}
      target={node.target}
      rel={node.rel}
    >
      {children}
    </a>
  )

case 'autolink':
  return (
    <a
      key={key}
      className="rich-link"
      href={node.url}
      target="_blank"
      rel="noopener noreferrer"
    >
      {children}
    </a>
  )
```

**Key attributes:**
- Regular `link`: Uses stored `target` and `rel` attributes (respects user intent)
- `autolink`: Always opens in new tab with XSS protection (`rel="noopener noreferrer"`)

---

### 2. Link Cards (Block-Level Links)

Link cards are custom rich blocks displaying link previews.

#### Node Definition

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/LinkCardNode.ts`

**Class structure:**

```typescript
export class LinkCardNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
  __url: string
  __source?: string
  __id?: string
  __title?: string
  __description?: string
  __favicon?: string
  __image?: string

  static getType(): string {
    return 'link-card'
  }

  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    return createRendererDecoration('LinkCard', LinkCardRenderer, {
      url: this.__url,
      source: this.__source,
      id: this.__id,
      title: this.__title,
      description: this.__description,
      favicon: this.__favicon,
      image: this.__image,
    })
  }

  exportJSON(): SerializedLinkCardNode {
    return {
      ...super.exportJSON(),
      type: 'link-card',
      url: this.__url,
      source: this.__source,
      id: this.__id,
      title: this.__title,
      description: this.__description,
      favicon: this.__favicon,
      image: this.__image,
      version: 1,
    }
  }
}
```

**Pattern:** Uses the `DecoratorNode<ReactElement>` pattern with `createRendererDecoration()` helper (lines 163-173) to delegate rendering to a custom component while maintaining type safety.

#### Editor Rendering

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/components/renderers/LinkCardRenderer.tsx`

```typescript
export function LinkCardRenderer({
  url,
  title,
  description,
  favicon,
  image,
}: LinkCardRendererProps) {
  const displayTitle = title || url

  return (
    <a
      className="rich-link-card"
      href={url}
      target="_blank"
      rel="noopener noreferrer"
    >
      {image && (
        <span className="rich-link-card-image">
          <img src={image} alt="" loading="lazy" />
        </span>
      )}
      <span className="rich-link-card-content">
        <span className="rich-link-card-title">
          {favicon && (
            <img
              className="rich-link-card-favicon"
              src={favicon}
              alt=""
              width={16}
              height={16}
              onError={(e) => {
                ;(e.target as HTMLImageElement).style.display = 'none'
              }}
            />
          )}
          {displayTitle}
        </span>
        {description && (
          <span className="rich-link-card-description">{description}</span>
        )}
        <span className="rich-link-card-url">{url}</span>
      </span>
    </a>
  )
}
```

**Key features:**
- Favicon rendering with graceful fallback (hidden on load error)
- Image preview with lazy loading
- Fallback to URL if title missing
- Safe navigation with `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`

#### Static Renderer Rendering

The static renderer (`RichRenderer`) handles `link-card` nodes via the decoration pattern:

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-static-renderer/src/RichRenderer.tsx` (lines 95-105):

```typescript
if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
  try {
    const decoration = node.decorate(editor, editorConfig)
    if (decoration != null) {
      const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
      return applyBlockId(
        wrapDecoration(serialized, nodeKey, decoration),
        blockId,
        nodeKey,
      )
    }
  } catch {
    /* fallthrough to builtin */
  }
}
```

**Flow:**
1. Static renderer calls `node.decorate()` → returns `RendererWrapper` with `LinkCardRenderer`
2. `RendererWrapper` respects custom renderer injection via `RendererConfig` context
3. Falls back to default `LinkCardRenderer` if no override

---

### 3. DOM Decoration Pattern

**Reference pattern:** `HeadingAnchorPlugin` demonstrates post-render DOM manipulation.

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx`

```typescript
export function HeadingAnchorPlugin() {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()

  useEffect(() => {
    const usedSlugs = new Map<string, number>()

    return editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, (mutations) => {
      usedSlugs.clear()

      for (const [nodeKey, mutation] of mutations) {
        if (mutation === 'destroyed') continue

        const dom = editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)
        if (!dom) continue

        const text = dom.textContent || ''
        const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
        if (!baseSlug) continue

        const slug = deduplicateSlug(baseSlug, usedSlugs)
        dom.id = slug

        let anchor = dom.querySelector<HTMLAnchorElement>(
          '.rich-heading-anchor',
        )
        if (!anchor) {
          anchor = document.createElement('a')
          anchor.className = 'rich-heading-anchor'
          anchor.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')
          anchor.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1')
          dom.prepend(anchor)
        }
        anchor.href = `#${slug}`
      }
    })
  }, [editor])

  return null
}
```

**Key techniques for DOM decoration:**
1. `editor.registerMutationListener(NodeType, handler)` — react to node mutations
2. `editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)` — get DOM element corresponding to node
3. DOM manipulation via standard `document.createElement()` and `querySelector()`
4. Apply IDs, classes, aria attributes

---

### 4. Configuration & Registration

#### Renderer Config Type

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/types/renderer-config.ts`

```typescript
export interface RendererConfig {
  LinkCard?: ComponentType<LinkCardRendererProps>
  // ... other renderers
}
```

#### Node Registry

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/config.ts`

```typescript
export const customNodes: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>> = [
  LinkCardNode,
  // ... other custom nodes
]

export const allNodes: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>> = [
  ...builtinNodes,  // LinkNode, AutoLinkNode included
  ...customNodes,   // LinkCardNode
]
```

#### Static Renderer Context

The `RichRenderer` injects renderer config via context (lines 250-254 of `RichRenderer.tsx`):

```typescript
<RendererConfigProvider
  config={rendererConfig}
  mode="renderer"
  variant={variant}
>
  {/* content */}
</RendererConfigProvider>
```

Allows runtime override of `LinkCardRenderer` via `rendererConfig` prop.

---

### 5. Rendering Flow Comparison

#### Editor Mode

```
LinkCardNode (with edit plugins)
  ↓
decorate() → createRendererDecoration('LinkCard', LinkCardRenderer, props)
  ↓
RendererWrapper (reads RendererConfig context)
  ↓
LinkCardRenderer (or custom override)
```

#### Static Renderer Mode

```
RichRenderer (headless editor created)
  ↓
LinkCardNode.decorate() → RendererWrapper
  ↓
RendererWrapper (reads RendererConfig context)
  ↓
LinkCardRenderer (or custom override)
```

Both modes use the **same node and renderer** because `LinkCardNode.decorate()` is context-agnostic. Bundle optimization comes from not importing edit-only dependencies in static mode.

---

### 6. Summary Table

| Aspect | Inline Links | Link Cards |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| **Node** | `LinkNode`, `AutoLinkNode` (Lexical) | `LinkCardNode` (custom `DecoratorNode`) |
| **Editor Rendering** | Lexical's built-in link handling | `LinkCardRenderer` |
| **Static Rendering** | `case 'link'` in `renderBuiltinNode` | `node.decorate()` → `RendererWrapper` → `LinkCardRenderer` |
| **Output Element** | `<a href={url} ...>{children}</a>` | `<a className="rich-link-card" href={url} ...>{preview}</a>` |
| **Favicon Rendering** | N/A | `<img className="rich-link-card-favicon" onError={hide} />` |
| **DOM Decoration** | N/A (built-in) | Plugin pattern available (see `HeadingAnchorPlugin`) |
| **Custom Override** | Via Lexical (advanced) | Via `RendererConfig` context prop |

All file paths are absolute paths within `<REPO_ROOT>/`.

## Task 1: Checkbox UI Style

The current checkbox style is in `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts` lines 227-279. It uses `::before` for the checkbox box and `::after` for the checkmark. The user wants to change it to a DaisyUI-style checkbox with:

- `appearance: none`
- `border-radius: var(--radius-selector)` 
- Box shadow effects
- A `::before` pseudo-element with clip-path animation for the checkmark


- Transition effects

The key challenge here is that the current implementation relies on both `::before` and `::after` pseudo-elements on the `<li>` tag—one for the box itself and one for the checkmark—but the DaisyUI approach consolidates this differently. Since these are Lexical checklist items rendered as `<li>` elements with CSS-generated visuals, I need to restructure how the pseudo-elements work while maintaining the same DOM structure.

    12→    .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    13→}
    14→
    15→function extractText(node: any): string {
    16→  if (node.text) return node.text
    17→  if (node.children) return node.children.map(extractText).join('')
    18→  return ''
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function renderBuiltinNode(
    22→  node: any,
    23→  key: string,
    24→  children: ReactNode[] | null,
    25→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    26→): ReactNode {
    27→  switch (node.type) {
    28→    case 'root':
    29→      return <>{children}</>
    30→    case 'paragraph': {
    31→      const align = node.format
    32→        ? ({ textAlign: node.format } as const)
    33→        : undefined
    34→      return (
    35→        <p key={key} className="rich-paragraph" style={align}>
    36→          {children}
    37→        </p>
    38→      )
    39→    }
    40→    case 'heading': {
    41→      const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    42→      const text = extractText(node)
    43→      const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    44→      let slug = baseSlug
    45→      if (baseSlug) {
    46→        const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    47→        if (count !== undefined) {
    48→          slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
    49→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    50→        } else {
    51→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    52→        }
    53→      }
    54→      return (
    55→        <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
    56→          {slug && (
    57→            <a
    58→              className="rich-heading-anchor"
    59→              aria-hidden="true"
    60→              tabIndex={-1}
    61→              href={`#${slug}`}
    62→            />
    63→          )}
    64→          {children}
    65→        </Tag>
    66→      )
    67→    }
    68→    case 'quote':
    69→      return (
    70→        <blockquote key={key} className="rich-quote">
    71→          {children}
    72→        </blockquote>
    73→      )
    74→    case 'list': {
    75→      const Tag = node.listType === 'number' ? 'ol' : 'ul'
    76→      const cls =
    77→        node.listType === 'number'
    78→          ? 'rich-list-ol'
    79→          : node.listType === 'check'
    80→            ? 'rich-checklist rich-list-ul'
    81→            : 'rich-list-ul'
    82→      return (
    83→        <Tag
    84→          key={key}
    85→          className={cls}
    86→          start={node.start !== 1 ? node.start : undefined}
    87→        >
    88→          {children}
    89→        </Tag>
    90→      )
    91→    }
    92→    case 'listitem': {
    93→      const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
    94→      const hasNestedList = node.children?.some((c: any) => c.type === 'list')
    95→      let cls: string
    96→      if (hasNestedList) {
    97→        cls = 'rich-list-nested-item'
    98→      } else if (isChecklist) {
    99→        cls = node.checked
   100→          ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked'
   101→          : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked'
   102→      } else {
   103→        cls = 'rich-list-item'
   104→      }
   105→      return (
   106→        <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>
   107→          {children}
   108→        </li>
   109→      )
   110→    }
   111→    case 'link':
   112→      return (
   113→        <a
   114→          key={key}
   115→          className="rich-link"
   116→          href={node.url}
   117→          target={node.target}
   118→          rel={node.rel}
   119→        >
   120→          {children}
   121→        </a>
   122→      )
   123→    case 'autolink':
   124→      return (
   125→        <a
   126→          key={key}
   127→          className="rich-link"
   128→          href={node.url}
   129→          target="_blank"
   130→          rel="noopener noreferrer"
   131→        >
   132→          {children}
   133→        </a>
   134→      )
   135→    case 'horizontalrule':
   136→      return <hr key={key} className="rich-hr" />
   137→    case 'table':
   138→      return (
   139→        <div key={key} className={tableStyles.tableWrapper}>
   140→          <table className={tableStyles.table}>{children}</table>
   141→        </div>
   142→      )
   143→    case 'tablerow':
   144→      return <tr key={key}>{children}</tr>
   145→    case 'tablecell': {
   146→      const CellTag = node.headerState ? 'th' : 'td'
   147→      const cls = node.headerState
   148→        ? tableStyles.tableHead
   149→        : tableStyles.tableCell
   150→      return (
   151→        <CellTag
   152→          key={key}
   153→          className={cls}
   154→          colSpan={node.colSpan > 1 ? node.colSpan : undefined}
   155→        >
   156→          {children}
   157→        </CellTag>
   158→      )
   159→    }
   160→    case 'details': {
   161→      const summary = node.summary || ''
   162→      return (
   163→        <details
   164→          key={key}
   165→          className="rich-details"
   166→          open={node.open || undefined}
   167→        >
   168→          <summary className="rich-details-summary">
   169→            <span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span>
   170→            <span className="rich-details-chevron">
   171→              <svg
   172→                width="20"
   173→                height="20"
   174→                viewBox="0 0 20 20"
   175→                fill="none"
   176→                stroke="currentColor"
   177→                strokeWidth="1.5"
   178→                strokeLinecap="round"
   179→                strokeLinejoin="round"
   180→              >
   181→                <path d="M6 8L10 12L14 8" />
   182→              </svg>
   183→            </span>
   184→          </summary>
   185→          <div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div>
   186→        </details>
   187→      )
   188→    }
   189→    case 'spoiler':
   190→      return (
   191→        <span key={key} className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>
   192→          {children}
   193→        </span>
   194→      )
   195→    case 'ruby':
   196→      return createElement(RendererWrapper as any, {
   197→        key,
   198→        rendererKey: 'Ruby',
   199→        defaultRenderer: RubyRenderer,
   200→        props: {
   201→          reading: node.reading ?? '',
   202→          children,
   203→        },
   204→      })
   205→    case 'code':
   206→      return (
   207→        <pre key={key} className="rich-code-block">
   208→          <code>{children}</code>
   209→        </pre>
   210→      )
   211→    case 'code-highlight':
   212→      return <span key={key}>{node.text}</span>
   213→    case 'linebreak':
   214→      return <br key={key} />
   215→    case 'tab':
   216→      return <span key={key}>{'  '}</span>
   217→    default:
   218→      return null
   219→  }
   220→}
   221→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     2→import { HeadingNode } from '@lexical/rich-text'
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→
     5→function textToSlug(text: string): string {
     6→  return (
     7→    text
     8→      .toLowerCase()
     9→      .trim()
    10→      // eslint-disable-next-line regexp/no-dupe-characters-character-class
    11→      .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
    12→      .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
    13→      .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    14→  )
    15→}
    16→
    17→function deduplicateSlug(slug: string, usedSlugs: Map<string, number>): string {
    18→  const count = usedSlugs.get(slug)
    19→  if (count === undefined) {
    20→    usedSlugs.set(slug, 1)
    21→    return slug
    22→  }
    23→  usedSlugs.set(slug, count + 1)
    24→  return `${slug}-${count}`
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function HeadingAnchorPlugin() {
    28→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    29→
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    const usedSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    32→
    33→    return editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, (mutations) => {
    34→      usedSlugs.clear()
    35→
    36→      for (const [nodeKey, mutation] of mutations) {
    37→        if (mutation === 'destroyed') continue
    38→
    39→        const dom = editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)
    40→        if (!dom) continue
    41→
    42→        const text = dom.textContent || ''
    43→        const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    44→        if (!baseSlug) continue
    45→
    46→        const slug = deduplicateSlug(baseSlug, usedSlugs)
    47→        dom.id = slug
    48→
    49→        let anchor = dom.querySelector<HTMLAnchorElement>(
    50→          '.rich-heading-anchor',
    51→        )
    52→        if (!anchor) {
    53→          anchor = document.createElement('a')
    54→          anchor.className = 'rich-heading-anchor'
    55→          anchor.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')
    56→          anchor.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1')
    57→          dom.prepend(anchor)
    58→        }
    59→        anchor.href = `#${slug}`
    60→      }
    61→    })
    62→  }, [editor])
    63→
    64→  return null
    65→}
    66→
```
</details></tool-use>

    39→
    40→export function RichEditor({

    46→  onSubmit,

    58→}: RichEditorProps) {
    59→  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allEditNodes, ...extraNodes] : allEditNodes
    60→  setResolvedEditNodes(nodes)
    61→  const initialConfig = {

    63→    theme: editorTheme,

    74→
    75→  return (

    80→          mode="editor"
    81→          variant={variant}

   108→                  <SubmitShortcutPlugin onSubmit={onSubmit} />

   111→                  <KaTeXPlugin />

So I need to map DaisyUI's styles differently: the `.checkbox` visual styles go to `::before`, and the `.checkbox::before` checkmark styles go to `::after`, with the `<li>` handling all the click interactions.

I should put `cursor: pointer` on the `::before` to signal that the checkbox area is clickable, since Lexical's plugin likely detects clicks in that region to toggle the state.

Now I'm ready to write the actual CSS implementation.

Writing checkbox styles... Still writing checkbox styles... Still writing checkbox styles... Setting custom properties on list items... Still writing styles...

Now I'm thinking about the fallback state—should I display a globe icon when the favicon hasn't loaded yet? Looking at the reference implementation, it does use a globe as a fallback, so I should add that styling for the editor mode.

Writing SVG code...

Actually, I'm reconsidering the UX here—showing a fallback globe icon for every link without a favicon might feel cluttered. The user's request is specifically to guess the favicon when available, so I should just display it when found and show nothing otherwise, letting users add their own fallback behavior if needed. Looking back at the reference implementation, it seems the approach is to attempt favicon detection but gracefully handle missing ones.

So I'll adjust the strategy: set `data-favicon="none"` in the editor plugin when probing completes without finding anything, and `data-favicon="loaded"` when successful. The CSS will only render the favicon icon for the loaded state, keeping inline links clean.

Writing favicon styles...

Now I'm thinking through whether to display favicons for internal links—since the favicon probe only uses the hostname, internal paths won't have a full URL to probe against.

Actually, Lexical stores the complete URL in `LinkNode`, so even relative or same-host links will work fine. I could skip relative URLs without a protocol, but really I should just probe all valid http/https URLs. If it's pointing to the same site, showing that site's favicon is perfectly reasonable anyway, which is what the reference implementation does.

Let me start writing the code now.

One more thing to handle: the `data-favicon` attribute during Lexical reconciliation...

When Lexical updates the link node's DOM, it only modifies `href`, `rel`, `target`, and classes—it preserves data attributes and CSS variables. So my favicon attribute and CSS variable will stick around. But if the `href` changes, I need to re-probe the favicon. I'll track the previous href in a data attribute and skip re-probing if it hasn't changed, which avoids unnecessary work when the link updates for other reasons.

Time to implement this.

```typescript
const FAVICON_VARIANTS = [
  '/favicon.ico',
  '/favicon.png',
  '/favicon.svg',
  '/apple-touch-icon.png',
  '/apple-touch-icon-precomposed.png',
]

const faviconCache = new Map<string, string | null>()

export function getHostname(href: string): string | null {
  try {
    const url = new URL(href)
    if (url.protocol !== 'http:' && url.protocol !== 'https:') return null
    return url.hostname
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}

function probeImage(url: string): Promise<string | null> {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const img = new Image()
    img.onload = () => resolve(url)
    img.onerror = () => resolve(null)
    img.src = url
  })
}

export async function probeFavicon(
  hostname: string,
): Promise<string | null> {
  const cached = faviconCache.get(hostname)
  if (cached !== undefined) return cached

  for (const variant of FAVICON_VARIANTS) {
    const url = `https://${hostname}${variant}`
    const result = await probeImage(url)
    if (result) {
      faviconCache.set(hostname, result)
      return result
    }
  }

  faviconCache.set(hostname, null)
  return null
}

```

import { AutoLinkNode, LinkNode } from '@lexical/link'
import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
import { useEffect } from 'react'

import { getHostname, probeFavicon } from '../utils/favicon'

function applyFavicon(dom: HTMLElement, href: string) {
  if (dom.dataset.faviconHref === href) return
  dom.dataset.faviconHref = href

  const hostname = getHostname(href)
  if (!hostname) return

  probeFavicon(hostname).then((faviconUrl) => {
    if (faviconUrl) {
      dom.style.setProperty('--rc-link-favicon', `url(${faviconUrl})`)
      dom.dataset.favicon = 'loaded'
    }
  })
}

export function LinkFaviconPlugin() {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleMutations = (
      mutations: Map<string, 'created' | 'updated' | 'destroyed'>,
    ) => {
      for (const [nodeKey, mutation] of mutations) {
        if (mutation === 'destroyed') continue
        const dom = editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)
        if (!dom) continue
        const href = dom.getAttribute('href')
        if (href) applyFavicon(dom, href)
      }
    }

    const unregisterLink = editor.registerMutationListener(
      LinkNode,
      handleMutations,
    )
    const unregisterAutoLink = editor.registerMutationListener(
      AutoLinkNode,
      handleMutations,
    )

    return () => {
      unregisterLink()
      unregisterAutoLink()
    }
  }, [editor])

  return null
}

```

```tsx
'use client'

import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'

import { getHostname, probeFavicon } from '../utils/favicon'

export function LinkFavicon({ href }: { href: string }) {
  const [faviconUrl, setFaviconUrl] = useState<string | null>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    const hostname = getHostname(href)
    if (!hostname) return

    let cancelled = false
    probeFavicon(hostname).then((url) => {
      if (!cancelled) setFaviconUrl(url)
    })
    return () => {
      cancelled = true
    }
  }, [href])

  if (!faviconUrl) return null

  return (
    <img
      className="rich-link-favicon"
      src={faviconUrl}
      alt=""
      width={14}
      height={14}
      aria-hidden="true"
    />
  )
}

```

    42→      )
    43→    }
    44→    case 'heading': {
    45→      const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    46→      const text = extractText(node)
    47→      const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    48→      let slug = baseSlug
    49→      if (baseSlug) {
    50→        const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    51→        if (count !== undefined) {
    52→          slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
    53→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    54→        } else {
    55→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    56→        }
    57→      }
    58→      return (
    59→        <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
    60→          {slug && (
    61→            <a
    62→              className="rich-heading-anchor"
    63→              aria-hidden="true"
    64→              tabIndex={-1}
    65→              href={`#${slug}`}
    66→            />
    67→          )}
    68→          {children}
    69→        </Tag>
    70→      )
    71→    }
    72→    case 'quote':
    73→      return (
    74→        <blockquote key={key} className="rich-quote">
    75→          {children}
    76→        </blockquote>
    77→      )
    78→    case 'list': {
    79→      const Tag = node.listType === 'number' ? 'ol' : 'ul'
    80→      const cls =
    81→        node.listType === 'number'
    82→          ? 'rich-list-ol'
    83→          : node.listType === 'check'
    84→            ? 'rich-checklist rich-list-ul'
    85→            : 'rich-list-ul'
    86→      return (
    87→        <Tag
    88→          key={key}
    89→          className={cls}
    90→          start={node.start !== 1 ? node.start : undefined}
    91→        >
    92→          {children}
    93→        </Tag>
    94→      )
    95→    }
    96→    case 'listitem': {
    97→      const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
    98→      const hasNestedList = node.children?.some((c: any) => c.type === 'list')
    99→      let cls: string
   100→      if (hasNestedList) {
   101→        cls = 'rich-list-nested-item'
   102→      } else if (isChecklist) {
   103→        cls = node.checked
   104→          ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked'
   105→          : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked'
   106→      } else {
   107→        cls = 'rich-list-item'
   108→      }
   109→      return (
   110→        <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>
   111→          {children}
   112→        </li>
   113→      )
   114→    }
   115→    case 'link':
   116→      return (
   117→        <a
   118→          key={key}
   119→          className="rich-link"
   120→          href={node.url}
   121→          target={node.target || '_blank'}
   122→          rel={node.rel || 'noopener'}
   123→        >
   124→          <LinkFavicon href={node.url} />
   125→          {children}
   126→        </a>
   127→      )
   128→    case 'autolink':
   129→      return (
   130→        <a
   131→          key={key}
   132→          className="rich-link"
   133→          href={node.url}
   134→          target="_blank"
   135→          rel="noopener"
   136→        >
   137→          <LinkFavicon href={node.url} />
   138→          {children}
   139→        </a>
   140→      )
   141→    case 'horizontalrule':
   142→      return <hr key={key} className="rich-hr" />
   143→    case 'table':
   144→      return (
   145→        <div key={key} className={tableStyles.tableWrapper}>
   146→          <table className={tableStyles.table}>{children}</table>
   147→        </div>
   148→      )
   149→    case 'tablerow':
   150→      return <tr key={key}>{children}</tr>
   151→    case 'tablecell': {
   152→      const CellTag = node.headerState ? 'th' : 'td'
   153→      const cls = node.headerState
   154→        ? tableStyles.tableHead
   155→        : tableStyles.tableCell
   156→      return (
   157→        <CellTag
   158→          key={key}
   159→          className={cls}
   160→          colSpan={node.colSpan > 1 ? node.colSpan : undefined}
   161→        >
   162→          {children}
   163→        </CellTag>
   164→      )
   165→    }
   166→    case 'details': {
   167→      const summary = node.summary || ''
   168→      return (
   169→        <details
   170→          key={key}
   171→          className="rich-details"
   172→          open={node.open || undefined}
   173→        >
   174→          <summary className="rich-details-summary">
   175→            <span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span>
   176→            <span className="rich-details-chevron">
   177→              <svg
   178→                width="20"
   179→                height="20"
   180→                viewBox="0 0 20 20"
   181→                fill="none"
   182→                stroke="currentColor"
   183→                strokeWidth="1.5"
   184→                strokeLinecap="round"
   185→                strokeLinejoin="round"
   186→              >
   187→                <path d="M6 8L10 12L14 8" />
   188→              </svg>
   189→            </span>
   190→          </summary>
   191→          <div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div>
   192→        </details>
   193→      )
   194→    }
   195→    case 'spoiler':
   196→      return (
   197→        <span key={key} className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>
   198→          {children}
   199→        </span>
   200→      )
   201→    case 'ruby':
   202→      return createElement(RendererWrapper as any, {
   203→        key,
   204→        rendererKey: 'Ruby',
   205→        defaultRenderer: RubyRenderer,
   206→        props: {
   207→          reading: node.reading ?? '',
   208→          children,
   209→        },
   210→      })
   211→    case 'code':
   212→      return (
   213→        <pre key={key} className="rich-code-block">
   214→          <code>{children}</code>
   215→        </pre>
   216→      )
   217→    case 'code-highlight':
   218→      return <span key={key}>{node.text}</span>
   219→    case 'linebreak':
   220→      return <br key={key} />
   221→    case 'tab':
   222→      return <span key={key}>{'  '}</span>
   223→    default:
   224→      return null
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
```
</details></tool-use>

IDE diagnostic 均为 monorepo 内部包解析问题，两个包均已构建成功验证。以下为变更总结。

---

## 变更总结

### 1. Checkbox UI → DaisyUI 风格

**`packages/rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts`**

原样式以简单边框 + CSS transform 绘制勾勾，今改为 DaisyUI clip-path 动画风格：

- `::before` 绘制 checkbox 外框，用 `color-mix(in oklab, currentColor 20%, transparent)` 作边框色，`oklch` box-shadow 增加深度感，`--radius-selector` / `--depth` / `--size-selector` 等 DaisyUI CSS 变量自动继承主题
- `::after` 用 `clip-path: polygon(...)` + `rotate: 45deg` 绘制勾勾，unchecked 时 `opacity: 0` + 收缩 polygon，checked 时展开 polygon + `opacity: 1`，带 `0.3s` 过渡动画
- 大小由 `--rc-cb-size: calc(var(--size-selector, 0.1875rem) * 6)` 控制（默认 1.125rem）

### 2. Link Favicon

新增文件：
- **`packages/rich-editor/src/utils/favicon.ts`** — 共享 favicon 探测工具，依次尝试 `/favicon.ico`、`.png`、`.svg`、`/apple-touch-icon.png` 等变体，带内存缓存
- **`packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/LinkFaviconPlugin.tsx`** — 编辑器插件，监听 `LinkNode` / `AutoLinkNode` mutation，对每个链接 DOM 元素 probe favicon 后设 `--rc-link-favicon` CSS custom property + `data-favicon="loaded"` 属性
- **`packages/rich-editor/src/components/LinkFavicon.tsx`** — `'use client'` React 组件，供静态渲染器使用，用 `useEffect` 探测 favicon 后渲染 `<img>`

修改文件：
- **`RichEditor.tsx`** — 注册 `<LinkFaviconPlugin />`
- **`static-entry.ts`** — 导出 `LinkFavicon`
- **`renderBuiltinNode.tsx`** — `link` / `autolink` 节点内嵌 `<LinkFavicon>`
- **`shared.css.ts`** — `.rich-link[data-favicon="loaded"]::before` 显示 favicon（编辑器），`.rich-link-favicon` 样式化 `<img>`（静态渲染器）

---

    24→function $findLinkNode(node: LexicalNode): LinkNode | null {
    25→  let current: LexicalNode | null = node
    26→  while (current) {
    27→    if ($isLinkNode(current)) return current
    28→    current = current.getParent()
    29→  }
    30→  return null
    31→}
    32→
    33→interface LinkEditorState {
    34→  url: string
    35→  linkKey: string

    37→}
    38→
    39→export interface FloatingLinkEditorPluginProps {

    41→    url: string
    42→    linkKey: string
    43→    actionButtonClassName: string
    44→  }) => ReactNode
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function FloatingLinkEditorPlugin({
    48→  renderExtraActions,
    49→}: FloatingLinkEditorPluginProps = {}): ReactElement | null {

    51→  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)

    53→
    54→  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false)

    60→  const [inputUrl, setInputUrl] = useState('')

    62→  const updateLink = useCallback(() => {

    64→
    65→    const selection = $getSelection()
    66→    if (!$isRangeSelection(selection) || !selection.isCollapsed()) {
    67→      setVisible(false)

    70→
    71→    const anchorNode = selection.anchor.getNode()
    72→    const linkNode = $findLinkNode(anchorNode)

    75→      return
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    const url = linkNode.getURL()
    79→    const key = linkNode.getKey()

    83→    setInputUrl(url)

   114→

   118→      }
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    const onKeyDown = (e: globalThis.KeyboardEvent) => {

   124→    const onKeyUp = (e: globalThis.KeyboardEvent) => {
   125→      if (e.key === 'Meta' || e.key === 'Control') updateCursor(false)
   126→    }
   127→    const onBlur = () => updateCursor(false)
   128→
   129→    const onClick = (e: MouseEvent) => {
   130→      if (!(e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) return
   131→      const anchor = (e.target as HTMLElement).closest('a')
   132→      if (!anchor) return
   133→      const href = anchor.getAttribute('href')
   134→      if (href) {
   135→        e.preventDefault()
   136→        window.open(href, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')
   137→      }
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    root.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
   141→    root.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)

   143→    root.addEventListener('click', onClick)
   144→    return () => {

   146→      root.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)

   164→  }, [editor, inputUrl, linkState.linkKey])
   165→

   217→  return (

Base directory for this skill: <HOME>/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/systematic-debugging

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible → gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI → build → signing, API → service → database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets → workflow ✓, workflow → build ✗)

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes → Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script if no framework
   - MUST have before fixing
   - Use the `superpowers:test-driven-development` skill for writing proper failing tests

2. **Implement Single Fix**
   - Address the root cause identified
   - ONE change at a time
   - No "while I'm here" improvements
   - No bundled refactoring

3. **Verify Fix**
   - Test passes now?
   - No other tests broken?
   - Issue actually resolved?

4. **If Fix Doesn't Work**
   - STOP
   - Count: How many fixes have you tried?
   - If < 3: Return to Phase 1, re-analyze with new information
   - **If ≥ 3: STOP and question the architecture (step 5 below)**
   - DON'T attempt Fix #4 without architectural discussion

5. **If 3+ Fixes Failed: Question Architecture**

   **Pattern indicating architectural problem:**
   - Each fix reveals new shared state/coupling/problem in different place
   - Fixes require "massive refactoring" to implement
   - Each fix creates new symptoms elsewhere

   **STOP and question fundamentals:**
   - Is this pattern fundamentally sound?
   - Are we "sticking with it through sheer inertia"?
   - Should we refactor architecture vs. continue fixing symptoms?

   **Discuss with your human partner before attempting more fixes**

   This is NOT a failed hypothesis - this is a wrong architecture.

## Red Flags - STOP and Follow Process

If you catch yourself thinking:
- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "Add multiple changes, run tests"
- "Skip the test, I'll manually verify"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- "Pattern says X but I'll adapt it differently"
- "Here are the main problems: [lists fixes without investigation]"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- **"One more fix attempt" (when already tried 2+)**
- **Each fix reveals new problem in different place**

**ALL of these mean: STOP. Return to Phase 1.**

**If 3+ fixes failed:** Question the architecture (see Phase 4.5)

## your human partner's Signals You're Doing It Wrong

**Watch for these redirections:**
- "Is that not happening?" - You assumed without verifying
- "Will it show us...?" - You should have added evidence gathering
- "Stop guessing" - You're proposing fixes without understanding
- "Ultrathink this" - Question fundamentals, not just symptoms
- "We're stuck?" (frustrated) - Your approach isn't working

**When you see these:** STOP. Return to Phase 1.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Issue is simple, don't need process" | Simple issues have root causes too. Process is fast for simple bugs. |
| "Emergency, no time for process" | Systematic debugging is FASTER than guess-and-check thrashing. |
| "Just try this first, then investigate" | First fix sets the pattern. Do it right from the start. |
| "I'll write test after confirming fix works" | Untested fixes don't stick. Test first proves it. |
| "Multiple fixes at once saves time" | Can't isolate what worked. Causes new bugs. |
| "Reference too long, I'll adapt the pattern" | Partial understanding guarantees bugs. Read it completely. |
| "I see the problem, let me fix it" | Seeing symptoms ≠ understanding root cause. |
| "One more fix attempt" (after 2+ failures) | 3+ failures = architectural problem. Question pattern, don't fix again. |

## Quick Reference

| Phase | Key Activities | Success Criteria |
|-------|---------------|------------------|
| **1. Root Cause** | Read errors, reproduce, check changes, gather evidence | Understand WHAT and WHY |
| **2. Pattern** | Find working examples, compare | Identify differences |
| **3. Hypothesis** | Form theory, test minimally | Confirmed or new hypothesis |
| **4. Implementation** | Create test, fix, verify | Bug resolved, tests pass |

## When Process Reveals "No Root Cause"

If systematic investigation reveals issue is truly environmental, timing-dependent, or external:

1. You've completed the process
2. Document what you investigated
3. Implement appropriate handling (retry, timeout, error message)
4. Add monitoring/logging for future investigation

**But:** 95% of "no root cause" cases are incomplete investigation.

## Supporting Techniques

These techniques are part of systematic debugging and available in this directory:

- **`root-cause-tracing.md`** - Trace bugs backward through call stack to find original trigger
- **`defense-in-depth.md`** - Add validation at multiple layers after finding root cause
- **`condition-based-waiting.md`** - Replace arbitrary timeouts with condition polling

**Related skills:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - For creating failing test case (Phase 4, Step 1)
- **superpowers:verification-before-completion** - Verify fix worked before claiming success

## Real-World Impact

From debugging sessions:
- Systematic approach: 15-30 minutes to fix
- Random fixes approach: 2-3 hours of thrashing
- First-time fix rate: 95% vs 40%
- New bugs introduced: Near zero vs common
